channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Jimmy Carter, the 39th US president, has died at 100
item.description: ATLANTA (AP) — Jimmy Carter, the peanut farmer who won the presidency in the wake of the Watergate scandal and Vietnam War, endured humbling defeat after one tumultuous term and then redefined life after the White House as a global humanitarian, has died. He was 100 years old.
item.dc:creator: BILL BARROW and ALEX SANZ, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Fight over spending stopgap foreshadows funding challenges in new Congress
item.description: The messy government shutdown fight this month foreshadows some of the challenges House Republicans could face next year, as a major test on funding awaits the incoming GOP “trifecta.” Congress narrowly averted a government shutdown last week — but not without a bit of drama.
item.dc:creator: Aris Folley, NewsNation

item.title: Carter to be honored with events in Georgia, Washington
item.description: Former President Carter will be honored with public observances in both Georgia and Washington, D.C., in the coming days, following his death on Sunday. He will be interred in Plains, Ga., according to the Carter Center.
item.dc:creator: Katie Wadington, WGN 9

item.title: Significant milestones in life and career of Jimmy Carter
item.description: — Oct. 1, 1924: James Earl Carter Jr. is born in Plains, Georgia, son of James Sr. and Lillian Gordy Carter. — June 1946: Carter graduates from the U.S. Naval Academy. — July 1946: Carter marries Rosalynn Smith, in Plains.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Five defining moments of Jimmy Carter's presidency
item.description: Former President Carter's post-presidential life lasted decades, putting his career in the White House in a deep rearview mirror in many ways. Carter died Sunday, according to the Carter Center.
item.dc:creator: Stephen Neukam, NewsNation

item.title: Photos: Jimmy Carter, from the White House to building houses
item.description: From The White House to building houses, here's a look at former president Jimmy Carter's life in photos.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Former President Jimmy Carter dies at 100 years old
item.description: James Earl Carter, Jr., the 39th President of the United States and 76th governor of Georgia, has died at 100-years-old. Known from boyhood as Jimmy, Carter rose from the dusty peanut fields of Plains, Georgia, to the White House.
item.dc:creator: Tom Ingram, WGN 9

item.title: Jetliner skids off runway and bursts into flames while landing in South Korea, killing 179
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — A jetliner skidded off a runway, slammed into a concrete fence and burst into flames Sunday in South Korea after its landing gear apparently failed to deploy. All but two of the 181 people aboard were killed in one of the country’s worst aviation disasters, officials said.
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM and KIM TONG-HYUNG, Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: UnitedHealthcare taught us ways to deny claims: Former employee
item.description: Collins described crying at her desk while handling calls from desperate patients, as supervisors laughed.
item.dc:creator: Damita Menezes, NewsNation

item.title: Musk calls some MAGA supporters ‘contemptible fools’ as visa row intensifies
item.description: Tech billionaire Elon Musk labeled a section of President-elect Trump supporters as “contemptible fools” as the online debate around visas for highly skilled workers on the right intensifies.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, Fox 5

item.title: LIST: Top 10 wackiest Florida Man (and Woman) headlines of 2024
item.description: Another year has come and gone, but the wacky antics of Florida residents will live on forever through the internet.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Tucker, Fox 5

item.title: At least 15 people injured after Florida train hits fire truck
item.description: Three firefighters and 12 Brightline passengers were injured Saturday morning following a collision between a Delray Beach Fire Rescue truck and a train.
item.dc:creator: Sierra Rains, Fox 5

item.title: Big Lots reaches deal to keep hundreds of US stores open
item.description: The discount chain Big Lots, which filed for bankruptcy protection in September, has reached a deal that will keep hundreds of its stores and distribution centers open.
item.dc:creator: Dee-Ann Durbin, KRON4

item.title: Winning ticket for $1.22 billion lottery jackpot sold in California, Mega Millions says
item.description: At least one Mega Millions player has plenty of dough to ring in the New Year after drawing the winning number.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: These songs dominated the Billboard charts longest
item.description: Every now and then, a new song releases and dominates radio airwaves, Spotify playlists, and, of course, the esteemed Billboard Hot 100 chart—and in terms of those types of hits, 2024 has been pretty impressive.
item.dc:creator: Angela Underwood, Stacker, KRON4

item.title: NY correctional officers repeatedly struck handcuffed inmate, picked him up by his neck before he died: video
item.description: Correctional officers at a New York prison repeatedly punched a handcuffed inmate and lifted him by the neck before dropping him, newly released video shows.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Winning ticket for $1.22 billion lottery jackpot sold in California, Mega Millions says
item.description: At least one Mega Millions player has plenty of dough to ring in the New Year after drawing the winning number.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: US teacher jailed in Russia is formally designated as wrongfully detained
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — An American schoolteacher arrested in Russia on drug charges more than three years ago has been designated by the U.S. government as wrongfully detained, the State Department said Friday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Explosion at Tyson Foods plant leaves 1 dead, others injured
item.description: An explosion at a Tyson Foods plant in Georgia late Thursday night left one person dead and others injured.
item.dc:creator: Kyler Swaim, KRON4

item.title: Man indicted in burning death of woman inside a New York City subway train, prosecutors say
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The man accused of burning a sleeping woman to death inside a New York City subway train has been indicted on murder and arson charges, prosecutors announced Friday, as authorities continue working to identify the victim.
item.dc:creator: CEDAR ATTANASIO, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Economist says mass deportations will cost Mexico tens of billions of dollars, a blow to its economy
item.description: "It's going to be a blow just in terms of the number of people sent here, but what about all those countries that refuse to take in their own people, they will likely end up in Mexico, you have to care for them as well," he said.
item.dc:creator: Salvador Rivera, Fox 5

item.title: First severe human bird flu case shows mutations: CDC
item.description: Health experts are now warning pet owners to be cautious about raw foods and limit pets' exposure to wildlife.
item.dc:creator: Nancy Loo, NewsNation

item.title: Lava erupts from Hawaii's Kilauea volcano
item.description: One of the world's most active volcanoes spewed lava into the air for a second straight day on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Retailers, chains that announced closures in 2024
item.description: It was another tough year for restaurants and traditional brick-and-mortar retailers. Several national chains announced closures and bankruptcies in 2024 – not to mention the local small businesses that quietly shuttered around the country.
item.dc:creator: Celeste Houmard, Fox 5

item.title: Cat food distributed to Florida recalled after positive bird flu test; 1 cat dead
item.description: The product was sold through distributors in Arizona, California, Colorado, Florida, Georgia, Illinois, Maryland, Michigan, Minnesota, Pennsylvania, Rhode Island and Wisconsin.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump has pressed for voting changes. GOP majorities in Congress will try to make that happen
item.description: Republicans plan to move quickly in their effort to overhaul the nation’s voting procedures, seeing an opportunity with control of the White House and both chambers of Congress to push through long-sought changes that include voter ID and proof-of-citizenship requirements.
item.dc:creator: CHRISTINA A. CASSIDY Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Ukrainian refugee finds peace through Tarpon Springs color guard
item.description: The holidays bring a lot of happiness for many people, especially one Tarpon Springs High School student who is thankful she can celebrate in safety here in America.
item.dc:creator: Alessandra Young, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Biden gives life in prison to 37 of 40 federal death row inmates before Trump can resume executions
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Joe Biden on Monday announced that he is commuting the sentences of 37 of the 40 people on federal death row, converting their punishments to life imprisonment just weeks before President-elect Donald Trump, an outspoken proponent of expanding capital punishment, takes of
item.dc:creator: WILL WEISSERT and DARLENE SUPERVILLE, Associated Press, ABC 4
